Title: Speed up scheduling of synthetic drug chemicals under Poisons Act
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Authorities and regulators must fast-track the scheduling of synthetic drug chemicals under the Poisons Act 1952 to ensure unimpeded legal action against offenders.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said swift action was critical, warning that delays in gazetting these substances would severely hamper police investigations.
"When a police investigation traces a case back to synthetic drugs, the matter is referred to the Chemistry Department. If toxic elements are identified but happen to be missing from the poisons registry, it severely complicates any subsequent legal proceedings," he said.
He was speaking at a press conference after attending the Aspirasi Bebas Dadah Tour, organised by the National Anti-Drugs Agency (Nada), at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Puteri Wangsa today.
Saifuddin was responding to comments by Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay, who had called for a total ban on vapes following the emergence of a new synthetic drug known as "Piu Piu", which was found laced in e-cigarette liquids.
Ayob Khan had said the drug contains a mixture of Fentanyl and psychoactive chemicals, which can trigger a severe, zombie-like high in users.
Saifuddin said the issue of classifying toxic substances had been brought to the Cabinet to find a permanent solution.
Following this, the Home Ministry is stepping up coordination with the Health Ministry and the Chemistry Department to ensure more effective law enforcement against new synthetic drug syndicates.
He also revealed that national drug data mapping identified the country's East Coast as the hardest-hit area for synthetic drug use, with the trend starting in Kelantan and Terengganu before moving into Pahang.
While the West Coast still records the use of traditional substances such as marijuana and heroin, he noted that the East Coast is now dominated by synthetic drugs, which have a far more devastating impact.
Saifuddin said the country has recorded alarming statistics in domestic security, with a total of 192,000 individuals confirmed to be involved in drug and substance abuse nationwide.
This is according to an integrated data profile combining records from the Health Ministry, private rehabilitation centres, Nada rehab clinics, and the police narcotics division.
Based on the overall data, the national average stands at 560 drug abusers per 100,000 residents, with consumption trends heavily dominated by dangerous synthetic drugs.
Commenting on the situation in Johor, he said the state recorded an average of 499 people involved in drug abuse per 100,000 residents.
Although the state average sits below the national benchmark, Mersing recorded the highest rate in Johor at 1,200 individuals per 100,000 residents, followed by Pontian, Kota Tinggi, and Segamat.
He added that there are currently 30 drug rehabilitation centres nationwide managed by Nada, housing 5,000 clients.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Reset convention draws 20,000-strong crowd
Article:
TANAH MERAH: The inaugural 'Reset' Convention drew an overwhelming response today, with attendance reaching nearly 20,000 people — double the organisers' initial target.
Organisers from the 'Reset' movement had earlier projected a crowd of about 10,000 for the gathering, which was held at Kota Harmoni Expo Site here.
Tanah Merah member of parliament Datuk Seri Ikmal Hisham Abdul Aziz described the turnout as a strong show of support for the movement's aspirations and reform agenda.
"We are grateful and overwhelmed by the response. We initially expected around 10,000 attendees, but the turnout has reached almost 20,000.
"This gathering is not meant to attack or oppose anyone. It is a peaceful assembly for people to come together harmoniously and discuss the future of the country," he said.
Ikmal also expressed appreciation to Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang for agreeing to officiate the convention.
"We in Tanah Merah are very happy and deeply touched that Tan Sri Abdul Hadi was willing to attend and officiate this event," he said.
Abdul Hadi is expected to officially open the convention later today.
Among the prominent leaders present were Perikatan Nasional chairman and Terengganu Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar, Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud and Gerakan president Datuk Dr Dominic Lau Hoe Chai.
Also in attendance were Kelantan Pas commissioner Datuk Ahmad Yakub, former opposition leader and Reset movement founder Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin, former Bersatu Melaka chairman Tan Sri Abdul Rahim Thamby Chik and former Bersatu information chief Datuk Wan Saiful Wan Jan.
Other attendees included Masjid Tanah MP Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin, Beluran MP Datuk Seri Dr Ronald Kiandee and Machang MP Wan Ahmad Fayhsal Wan Ahmad Kamal.
The convention is expected to feature several key announcements on the Reset movement's future direction and political plans.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar approves immediate RM250,000 infrastructure allocation for Sipitang schools
Article:
SIPITANG: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today approved an immediate special allocation of RM250,000 for infrastructure development, including repairs on basic facilities at secondary schools in the Sipitang district.
He said the implementation of the assistance and the disbursement of funds would be coordinated immediately by the Implementation Coordination Unit of the Prime Minister's Department.
"For school facilities, I am approving RM250,000 immediately. I do not want a project to be approved in June 2026 and only begin in August 2027.
"That is an outdated attitude," he said when officiating at the groundbreaking ceremony of the Sabah Southern Madani Link project here today.
Also present were Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali, and Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud.
Anwar said representatives from the relevant agencies must visit the affected schools within two weeks to assess their needs and expedite the assistance for the comfort of teachers and students.
He also called on all Sabahans to ensure the relationship and cooperation between the federal and state governments remain strong to sustain development continuity.
Political stability at both the state and federal levels, he explained, was the most important pillar enabling the government to focus fully on improving public welfare.
Anwar stressed that the time had come to end prolonged political disputes as they only harmed the state and deterred foreign investment.
"That is why I think enough is enough with all these disputes over various issues. The country's politics must be stable. If there is no stability, people will not invest and we cannot focus on development," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Don't rely on opposition's weaknesses to win state polls, Fahmi tells PH
Article:
KUALA PILAH: Pakatan Harapan (PH) must rely on its own strengths rather than banking on the opposition's weaknesses to secure victories in the upcoming Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections.
PH communications director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the opposition's vulnerabilities should not compromise the coalition's election machinery preparations or lead to complacency.
"A weak opponent does not mean we are strong. The leadership has directed all Pakatan Harapan machinery and teams to build solid ground.
"We must possess our own distinct strengths and not lean on the shortcomings of our rivals or any other parties in this struggle," he told reporters after visiting the National Information Dissemination Centre (Nadi) in Seri Menanti today.
Fahmi, who is also the communications minister, was commenting on PH's confidence ahead of the dual state polls.
The PKR central leadership council member said the coalition would hold fast to this philosophy while continuing to assess shifting political dynamics.
"We must evaluate political dynamics from time to time, but Pakatan Harapan remains our core foundation," he said.
Fahmi added that PH would ensure its party machinery and communications teams move swiftly, campaign effectively with facts, and recognise that modern voters consume information across multiple channels.
"Voters rely on both mass media and social media. Our teams must have the capability to engage voters effectively beyond face-to-face interactions.
"Seat allocations for Johor and Negri Sembilan have been finalised. Candidate screening is underway and will be concluded soon, with names expected to be announced within a week," he said.
On the potential spread of fake news and fabricated party logos during the campaign period, Fahmi said his ministry would collaborate with the Malaysian Media Council to curb misinformation, particularly on social media platforms.
"Social media platforms must take immediate action. We have already observed state election-related misinformation on platforms like TikTok.
"We will hold discussions with the Malaysian Media Council and plan to meet with social media platform providers to coordinate our response. I hope to make an announcement regarding this ahead of National Journalists' Day," he said.
The Election Commission has scheduled the Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections to run on separate timelines.
For Johor, nomination day is set for June 27, followed by early voting on July 7 and polling day on July 11.
Nomination day for Negri Sembilan is scheduled for July 18, with early voting on July 28 and polling day on August 1.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hazmat team deployed after chemical spill in Kuantan
Article:
KUANTAN: A hazardous materials (Hazmat) team was deployed after a tanker carrying hydrochloric acid overturned near the Cherating bridge here today, causing part of its load to spill into a nearby mangrove swamp.
The incident also left the lorry driver injured.
Pahang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Mohd Salahuddin Isa said the Gebeng fire station received an alert at 7.32am and dispatched nine firefighters to the scene.
"The lorry, which was transporting the chemical from Pasir Gudang, Johor, to Kemaman, Terengganu, landed on its side, resulting in a minor leak.
"Firefighters were informed that the injured driver had been taken to Kemaman Hospital for treatment," he said in a statement.
Salahuddin said firefighters inspecting the site found a leak from the storage tank's manhole cover, with hydrochloric acid flowing into a nearby mangrove swamp.
"A water curtain was deployed to prevent acid vapours from spreading towards the main road.
"The Department of Environment and the company involved were notified to assist with the removal of the chemical, while the Hazmat team monitored the situation at the site. The volume of the spill is estimated to be less than 1,000 litres," he said.
Salahuddin said a crane would be used to lift the overturned tanker before the remaining chemical was transferred to a replacement tanker.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Asyraf Wajdi says poor students will get help, urges them to accept offers
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki has urged students from poor and underprivileged backgrounds not to reject university offers due to financial concerns, assuring them that assistance is available.
Asyraf said he had received numerous messages from students, including orphans and those from low-income families, who were considering turning down public university offers because of the costs involved.
He said he had since contacted several vice-chancellors and discussed the matter with Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir, who instructed universities to assist students facing financial difficulties.
"I have received many messages on social media regarding orphans and students from poor families who are considering rejecting offers from public universities.
"I have contacted several vice-chancellors, and Minister Dr Zambry has instructed universities to help students facing financial difficulties. Do not reject the offers," he said in a Facebook post today.
Asyraf also assured students who received offers from Mara institutions, including Kolej Mara, Kolej Profesional Mara, TVET Mara, Kolej Poly-Tech Mara, Universiti Poly-Tech Malaysia and Universiti Kuala Lumpur, that support would be provided to those in need.
He said education remained a key pathway to transforming lives and breaking the cycle of poverty, adding that Mara would continue supporting deserving students.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Sabah southern link project reflects federal commitment to Sabah's interior, says PM
Article:
SIPITANG:  The Sabah Southern Madani Link (SSML) electricity grid project reflects the federal government's commitment to ensuring infrastructure development reaches communities in Sabah's interior.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the project was in line with the Malaysia Madani policy, which emphasises balanced and equitable development rather than concentrating progress solely in major urban centres.
He stressed that development could not have a meaningful impact if basic amenities remained inaccessible to communities in remote areas, particularly in Sabah's interior.
"That is why I am determined to ensure that development is distributed across all parts of the state, including rural and interior areas.
"This is what distinguishes our approach from that of some other countries, where development is concentrated only in major cities," he said when officiating the groundbreaking ceremony for the SSML project here.
Also present were Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali, and Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud.
Anwar said many of those criticising development allocations for Sabah and Sarawak had never visited the ground to witness firsthand the challenges faced by local communities, including poor road conditions and issues related to water and electricity supply.
"It is our responsibility to explain this to them and ensure that priority continues to be given to these allocations to guarantee essential amenities for the people and drive industrial growth here," he said.
Meanwhile, the prime minister said the implementation of the SSML project would also catalyse a major transformation of Sabah's power grid, enabling the state to play a significant role in realising the Asean Power Grid initiative aimed at linking electricity supply systems across Southeast Asia.
He said the integration would involve the development of a regional energy interconnection network stretching from Vietnam via subsea cables to Peninsular Malaysia and Singapore, before being linked with Sarawak's subsea power network.
"With the enhanced capacity of the Sabah Grid, we will be able to connect electricity supply from Sarawak to Sabah and channel it onward to Kalimantan in Indonesia and subsequently to the southern region," he said.
Anwar said the development of an integrated power grid would not only improve the reliability of electricity supply in Sabah, but also bring significant benefits to the people through better infrastructure, a more stable energy supply and stronger appeal to investors.
"Who would want to invest if our areas are left in the dark or if the power supply is insufficient to support industry? That is why this international-scale infrastructure is being developed to ensure the state's economic growth can be accelerated more effectively," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hundreds gather at Thai royal palace to bid farewell to Princess Bajrakitiyabha
Article:
BANGKOK: Hundreds of black-clad Thai mourners gathered at the royal palace in Bangkok on Saturday, awaiting the arrival of the body of the king's eldest daughter a day after her death was announced.
Princess Bajrakitiyabha, known as Princess Bha, died aged 47 on Thursday after spending more than three years in a coma due to a cardiac condition.
"I will wait here all day for her body to arrive," Donnapha Kladbupha, a 54-year-old English teacher and self-described royalist, told AFP.
"When it comes to saying goodbye, it's not easy for us," she said, adding that the monarchy represented "unity" for Thai people during times of distress.
Donnapha joined around 20 fellow members of an online royalist group in what she called an effort "to give support to my king."
Nearby, mourners queued for their turn to perform a Buddhist ritual of pouring holy water into a ceremonial bowl placed before a portrait of the princess.
"I'm sad that she passed away, especially when I saw her pictures as a child," another mourner, Nitikan Tephakham, 79, from the northeastern province of Roi Et, told AFP.
"When she was sick, I prayed for sacred beings to protect her and hoped for a miracle," she added.
- STRICT PROTOCOL -
The princess' body is due to be transferred from Chulalongkorn Hospital, where she was treated, to the Grand Palace on Saturday afternoon – a customary procedure for deceased members of the royal family.
Thai police and public transport operators warned people to expect heavy traffic along the procession route and advised motorists to avoid affected areas where possible.
When King Bhumibol Adulyadej passed away in October 2016, his body was also taken to the Grand Palace, where it lay in state before an elaborate royal cremation ceremony the following year.
The Thai government has yet to announce details of the princess' funeral, but it has instructed state officials to wear black and ordered that flags be flown at half-mast for 15 days.
The proceedings were carried out under tight security and strict protocol, where attendants wore formal attire, media access was restricted and dozens of police officers stood guard.
Tourists at the palace grounds, one of Bangkok's most popular attractions, also had to find workarounds.
"It's something quite unique that someone can be this much loved," Maria Marcais, a 22-year-old visitor from Canada.
"It's profound," she said.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Azalina to seek 2027 Budget allocation to expand rural legal aid satellite offices
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said will seek an allocation under the 2027 Budget to expand Legal Aid Department (JBG) satellite offices to rural areas nationwide.
She said the move is important to ensure that the public, particularly those in remote and rural areas, have easier access to legal aid services and are aware of their rights to legal protection and assistance.
"When we talk about legal aid, we must provide protection to the people so that they know they have the right to obtain legal assistance.
"That is why I will apply for an allocation in next year's budget. If we can establish more satellite offices in districts located far from state offices, it will make it easier for the public to access legal aid services," she told reporters after officiating the JBG Satellite Office at the Pengerang Court in Bandar Penawar here today.
Azalina, who is also the Pengerang member of parliament, said the service would be further strengthened through the use of JBG mobile vans under the Justice on Wheels (JOW) programme to reach villages and remote areas, including in Sabah and Sarawak.
She said the approach is in line with the government's aspiration to bring legal aid services directly to the people rather than waiting for them to visit JBG offices.
"For me, there is no point amending the Legal Aid and Public Defence Bill if people do not know where to seek protection and assistance.
"I have also instructed the director-general to ensure that these services are provided in all areas, including constituencies represented by the opposition. We will send letters to every member of parliament so that they can choose suitable dates according to their respective states," she said.
Earlier in her speech, Azalina said the opening of the JBG Pengerang Satellite Office marked a strategic step by the Madani government in strengthening access to justice through legal aid services that are closer, more accessible, and more responsive to the needs of the people.
She said the establishment of the satellite office, which resulted from a collaboration with the Office of the Chief Registrar of the Federal Court of Malaysia, would reduce the need for members of the public to travel long distances, thereby saving time and costs.
"The opening of the JBG Pengerang Satellite Office will bring legal aid services closer to the people, in line with the Madani government's commitment to enhancing access to justice, because justice is not a privilege but a right of every citizen, ensuring that no one is left behind by the nation's justice system," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia ready to deploy peacekeepers if UN extends Unifil mandate, says Khaled
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Malaysia may continue deploying a peacekeeping force to Lebanon under the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil) mission if required.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the matter is being discussed with the United Nations (UN) following Lebanon's deteriorating security situation due to the ongoing conflict involving Israel.
"Malaysia had previously planned to end its peacekeeping involvement as soon as the Unifil mandate expires this December.
"The National Security Council (NSC) previously decided that the Unifil mandate would end in December, meaning we would bring everyone back.
"However, Israel's continuous attacks have raised questions about whether the UN peacekeeping force needs to be maintained in Lebanon to prevent the security situation in the country from worsening," he said.
"Now, the NSC is considering what to do regarding Lebanon, and if it is decided that Unifil needs to continue, Malaysia is ready to be involved in sending a force," he told a press conference after launching the ministry's Jiwa Murni Madani programme at SK Johor Kampong here today.
Khaled said UN representatives had held discussions with Malaysia regarding the future of the Unifil mission and the need to increase the preparedness level of the peacekeepers.
"We received a UN representative for discussions, and we talked about several matters. First was on ensuring our personnel have better preparation than what is currently in place.
"Instead of just being peacekeepers, they are now more exposed to being in the middle of conflict and war. That is why we discussed the need for better preparation for our personnel," he said.
Commenting on the injuries sustained by two Malaysian Battalion (Malbatt) personnel recently, he said the members only suffered minor injuries from explosion shrapnel.
"The injuries suffered were not serious and treatment was given in Lebanon as our force has its own medical facilities there.
"We are under UN command. We have been given guidelines that such threats and challenges can indeed happen. So, we have advised them to take precautionary actions. We are grateful that there were no incidents involving lives, as has happened to some other peacekeeping forces," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Respect your friends', Zahid tells DAP Youth chief
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has reminded coalition partners to be respectful in public discourse after a DAP leader labelled the party hypocritical.
The remark came after DAP Youth chief Woo Kah Leong described Umno as hypocritical and arrogant over Johor Umno's plan to contest the upcoming Johor election on its own rather than cooperate with DAP.
Woo argued that Johor Umno's position contradicted the current political reality, given that the party remains part of the unity government.
Zahid said political partners should avoid making reckless statements that could strain relationships within the coalition.
"They must learn to respect their friends. Do not make careless remarks.
"I do not think even the secretary-general (Anthony Loke) of his party behaves in such a manner.
"He must respect the principles of political partnership," he said after attending the 4th Southeast Asian Scholars Conference 2026 at the Kuala Lumpur Convention Centre today.
Woo had also alleged that Umno was being hypocritical by seeking cooperation with Pakatan Harapan at the federal level while adopting a different approach in certain elections.
Zahid said mutual respect was essential in maintaining cooperation among political partners.
"Political partners should look after each other's feelings and, most importantly, guard their words so that they do not simply say whatever is in their hearts," he said.
"Even if there is a tiger in your heart, let only a goat come out. That is what they should learn."
Yesterday, the Election Commission (EC) set July 11 as polling day for the Johor election.
EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said nomination day and early voting have been fixed for June 27 and July 7 respectively.
The Johor legislative assembly was dissolved on June 1 after caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced that he had obtained the consent of the Johor Regent, Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Reset movement to announce new party, Dewan Rakyat plan today
Article:
TANAH MERAH: The Reset movement is expected to announce several major developments, including the formation of a new political party and a parliamentary arrangement ahead of the next Dewan Rakyat sitting.
Tasik Gelugor member of parliament and former Bersatu leader Datuk Wan Saiful Wan Jan said the announcements would be made by Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang and Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin.
"I am here as one of those involved in the Reset movement. Today, several important announcements will be made.
"God willing, we will announce details on a new party formed through the Reset movement, as well as the arrangement in the Dewan Rakyat for the upcoming parliamentary sitting," he told the New Straits Times when met at the Reset Convention here today.
However, Wan Saiful declined to reveal further details, saying he did not want to pre-empt Abdul Hadi and Hamzah.
"I cannot get ahead of Tok Guru Haji Hadi and Datuk Seri Hamzah. Let them make the announcement themselves," he said.
The remarks have intensified speculation over the future direction of the Reset movement, which has drawn participation from several former Bersatu leaders and members who were expelled, suspended or removed from the party.
The convention this afternoon, attended by political leaders, professionals and supporters, is expected to outline the movement's political roadmap ahead of upcoming elections and the next parliamentary sitting.
The event is expected to be officiated by Hadi, with about 15,000 supporters in attendance.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fadhlina: Govt committed to supporting all education streams fairly
Article:
GEORGE TOWN:  The government remains committed to supporting all education pathways fairly and inclusively, regardless of institution or background, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said today.
"That is why, in the context of providing pathways for Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) students, we also ensure commitments to strengthening the Malay language and History are well-received.
"All groups must be given due consideration, not only UEC students, but also others, including those from institutions outside the national education stream.
"We want to support all education streams so that our education system is elevated on an equal basis, ensuring fairness for all and recognising that education is for Malaysian children," she said.
Fadhlina said this in her speech at the handing over of the approval letter for the construction of the new SJKC Heng Ee building here.
She outlined the Madani administration's approach to education reform, ensuring that all streams receive appropriate recognition and support in line with national aspirations, while strengthening core subjects like Bahasa Melayu and History.
Fadhlina added that education must be treated as a shared national agenda involving all stakeholders, including schools, parent-teacher associations, and education boards, to ensure long-term progress and that no student is left behind.
It was previously reported that UEC holders and tahfiz graduates applying for specific public higher education programmes remain subject to university-set conditions and current government policies.
Malaysian Qualifications Agency (MQA) chief executive officer Professor Datuk Dr Mohammad Shatar Sabran had said applicants must also meet the mandatory requirement of
passing Bahasa Melayu and History
at the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) level.
On the new school building, Fadhlina said it would be constructed in Bayan Baru, with the school having successfully raised over RM20 million for the project.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Set the date': Albert Tei accepts J-KOM chief's debate challenge
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Businessman and social media personality Albert Tei today accepted a debate challenge from Community Communications Department (J-Kom) director-general Mohd Hisyamuddin Ghazali.
"I am ready," he said, urging Hisyamuddin to set a date, venue and moderator.
Speaking at a protest outside TikTok Malaysia's office today, Tei said he was ready to face the debate following remarks allegedly made by Hisyamuddin when they met outside the Putrajaya district police headquarters earlier this week.
"On Thursday morning in Putrajaya, he challenged me to a debate, and many members of the media were there.
"I am accepting the challenge. I want to know the venue, who will host it, what time and what date," he said.
Tei also urged Hisyamuddin not to withdraw from the proposed debate, saying the public would expect the challenge to be honoured.
The remarks were made during the "Himpunan Gegar TikTok" gathering outside TikTok Malaysia's office in Sunway Velocity, where participants protested what they claimed were restrictions imposed on online content critical of the government.
Earlier,
Hisyamuddin lodged a police report
against Tei for allegedly causing a disturbance at a government premises.
The report, lodged at the Putrajaya district police headquarters, also accused Tei of making baseless allegations against the Communications Ministry and urged police to investigate the matter.
Hisyamuddin said Tei's actions in entering a government premises to provoke, disrupt official duties and create anxiety among civil servants could not be taken lightly.
As Hisyamuddin was addressing the media, Tei unexpectedly appeared at the scene.
"While I was holding a press conference on the police report, he suddenly showed up. I approached him and answered all his questions. I even gave him my phone number and invited him to a debate," Hisyamuddin said in a Facebook post accompanied by a video.
In the video, Tei could be heard congratulating Hisyamuddin on his appointment as J-Kom director-general.
"How are you? Doing well? Congratulations on your appointment. We are professionals, right?" Tei said as the two shook hands.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No utility services for illegal structures, says Nga
Article:
IPOH: Buildings constructed without planning permission are illegal and will not be granted water or electricity supplies.
Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said this is to prevent the emergence of illegal settlements, including those linked to foreign communities such as the Rohingya.
"The issue of Rohingya refugees falls under the jurisdiction of the Immigration Department, which conducts regular inspections and enforcement operations.
"However, the government's policy is clear. Anyone entering the country, whether on humanitarian grounds or as a refugee, must obtain government approval," he told reporters after visiting an unclaimed money mobile counter here today.
Nga was commenting on reports of an unauthorised four-storey building constructed by the Rohingya community in a remote area of Sungai Tekali, Hulu Langat, Selangor.
Meanwhile, he said the government has revoked 26,108 business licences operated by foreign nationals through 4,000 enforcement operations carried out nationwide as of May this year.
"Foreign nationals are not allowed to run businesses or hold business licences. They may work as employees, but they cannot own businesses," he said.
Nga added that local authorities nationwide have been directed to step up enforcement efforts, while urging the public to report the presence of illegal immigrants or any suspicious activities.
On the unclaimed money campaign, he said RM13 billion is currently available for claim and encouraged the public to check their status.
"The process is simple. Citizens only need to bring their identity card and a bank statement," he said.
Nga said the initiative is in line with Section 13(1) of the Unclaimed Moneys Act 1965, under which funds left untouched for more than seven years are transferred to the Unclaimed Moneys Account managed by the Accountant General's Department.
"The money belongs to the rightful owners, but many are unaware that they have unclaimed funds. Starting this week, we will launch the campaign beginning in the Kepayang state constituency, with Pakatan Harapan elected representatives visiting constituencies across Perak to assist the public in reclaiming their money," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Over 54,000 Johor oil palm smallholders secure MSPO certification
Article:
BATU PAHAT: More than 54,000 Johor oil palm smallholders have obtained the Malaysian Sustainable Palm Oil (MSPO) certification, strengthening the country's position in the global market.
Plantation and Commodities Minister Datuk Seri Dr Noraini Ahmad said over 16,000 smallholders in Batu Pahat alone had secured the certification, reflecting strong participation in the government's sustainability agenda.
She said MSPO certification is increasingly important amid global economic uncertainty, geopolitical tensions, and supply chain disruptions, as it serves as proof of Malaysia's commitment to responsible production.
"MSPO is not merely a certification. It demonstrates Malaysia's commitment to sustainable and responsible palm oil production while strengthening confidence among international markets.
"It also serves as an important shield against unfounded allegations and negative perceptions directed at the country's palm oil industry," she said after officiating the Batu Pahat Oil Palm Smallholders MSPO Strengthening programme at Dewan Peladang Sri Medan here today.
Noraini said smallholders remain the backbone of the national palm oil industry, contributing significantly to local economies and rural livelihoods.
To enhance productivity and incomes, she said the government will continue providing support through the Oil Palm Smallholders Replanting Financing Incentive Scheme 2.0 (TSPKS 2.0), Tunas advisory services, crop disease management programmes, and the promotion of good agricultural practices.
In Batu Pahat, 222 applications under TSPKS 2.0 have been approved, covering more than 430 hectares of plantation land.
She added that nine estate road projects for smallholders had also been approved with allocations exceeding RM1.8 million to improve the transportation of fresh fruit bunches and operational efficiency.
Noraini, who is also the Parit Sulong member of parliament, said the government remains committed to ensuring no smallholder is left behind in efforts to modernise and strengthen the sector.
"When smallholders progress, local economies will grow, and the country's palm oil industry will become even stronger," she said.
The programme, organised by the Malaysian Palm Oil Board (MPOB) in collaboration with the Malaysian Sustainable Palm Oil Council (MSPO), aimed to raise awareness and encourage wider adoption of the certification.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No question of betrayal after term ends, says Zahid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi rejected claims that the party betrayed its allies by choosing to contest separately in the Negri Sembilan election, saying there was no question of disloyalty once a government's term had ended.
He said Barisan Nasional's support for the Negri Sembilan government remained in place until the end of its term and that the coalition would continue working within the political alliance while seeking to strengthen its position in the state assembly.
"There is no betrayal because our support for the government in Negri Sembilan is until the end of the term.
"When the dissolution was announced, we worked as part of a coalition that has its own direction.
"I do not think there is any betrayal when the term of a state or national administration has ended. We will find the best way to strengthen our position in the state assembly or in Parliament," he told reporters.
Zahid was responding to criticism from Pakatan Harapan leaders, who described Umno's decision to go it alone in the Negri Sembilan election as an act of betrayal.
On criticism by DAP deputy chairman Nga Kor Ming over the election date, Zahid said the matter was entirely at the discretion of the Election Commission (EC).
"That is the right and power of the Election Commission.
"No individual, especially those who have served as members of the cabinet, should express such personal views. More so if the person is a senior leader of a party that is part of the government," he said.
The Negri Sembilan election will be held on Aug 1, with nomination day set for July 18 and early voting on July 28.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Timor-Leste President Jose Ramos-Horta arrives in Malaysia for five-day special visit
Article:
SEPANG: Timor-Leste President Jose Ramos-Horta arrived in Malaysia today for a five-day special visit.
Ramos-Horta and his delegation arrived at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 1 at 11.12am on a commercial flight.
Upon arrival, he received a static guard of honour mounted by the First Battalion Royal Ranger Regiment, commanded by Captain Luqman Hakim Abd Khar, at the Bunga Raya Complex.
Also present to welcome the president were Wisma Putra Chief of Protocol Ikram Mohd Ibrahim and Ceremonial and International Conference Secretariat Division Chief of Ceremony Datuk Rozainor Ramli.
On Monday, Ramos-Horta is scheduled to attend a dialogue session titled "A Changing World, Enduring Values" at Taylor's University.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will host a luncheon in his honour at the Seri Perdana Complex on Tuesday.
The president will also attend a book launch ceremony and deliver a distinguished lecture at an event organised by the Institute of Diplomacy and Foreign Relations (IDFR).
Ramos-Horta is scheduled to depart for Dili on June 17.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Protesters warn of larger rallies if alleged TikTok restrictions continue
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Protesters outside TikTok Malaysia's office have warned of larger demonstrations if the platform continues what they claim are restrictions on content critical of the government.
The gathering, dubbed "Himpunan Gegar TikTok", involved social media personalities, content creators, and politicians, who accused the platform of unfairly restricting accounts and content expressing dissenting views.
Businessman Albert Tei, a critic of the government, said the rally was intended to send a message to TikTok Malaysia over alleged restrictions.
"I consider this a declaration of war against TikTok. If they continue blocking accounts, there will be Himpunan Gegar TikTok 2, Himpunan Gegar TikTok 3 and more gatherings in the future," he said.
Meanwhile, Bersatu Youth executive council member Na'im Brundage described the protest as the beginning of a broader campaign to pressure TikTok Malaysia and the government over the issue.
"If TikTok Malaysia and the government continue restricting online content and accounts that criticise them, we will organise more gatherings like this and increase the pressure," he said.
Na'im said social media platforms should apply transparent and consistent policies when taking action against users.
He also questioned TikTok's "aggressive" approach towards accounts purportedly critical of the government.
The protest was held under police monitoring.
The New Straits Times is reaching out to TikTok Malaysia for comment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Defence Ministry orders tighter security for Malbatt after Lebanon airstrike incident
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: The Defence Ministry has instructed Malaysian Battalion (Malbatt) 850-13 personnel to tighten security measures following an airstrike that left two peacekeepers with minor injuries in Lebanon on Thursday.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the directive was crucial to ensure the safety of Malaysian peacekeepers serving in the conflict zone.
"We have provided guidance that such threats and challenges can indeed occur, and we have advised the contingent to take precautionary measures," he said after officiating at the ministry's Jiwa Murni Madani Programme at Sekolah Kebangsaan Johor Kampong here today.
He clarified that the injuries were caused by bomb splinters landing near the personnel's location rather than a direct strike near Haris in southern Lebanon.
"All injured personnel received treatment from the medical team on duty there, and their condition is stable," he said.
Khaled added that the United Nations Security Council is reassessing the deployment of peacekeeping forces in Lebanon, as the mandate for the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil) is scheduled to expire in December.
"The government's current plan is to bring all Malbatt personnel home by the end of this year.
"However, if the UN decides that the presence of peacekeeping forces should continue to prevent Lebanon from descending into complete devastation, Malaysia is prepared to consider such cooperation," he said.
He also expressed gratitude that there have been no fatalities involving Malaysian personnel, unlike the casualties suffered by peacekeeping contingents from other nations in the conflict area.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Greatest courage': Fadhlina praises PM's education policies
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek today praised the Madani administration's commitment to inclusive education policies, saying they ensure no group is left behind.
She described the government as one that had shown courage in implementing policies aimed at uplifting all Malaysians without marginalising any community.
"This is the government and prime minister who have shown the greatest courage in defending education policies that uplift all without marginalising anyone," she said at a ceremony to hand over an approval letter for the construction of the new SJKC Heng Ee building here.
Fadhlina said the government remained steadfast in strengthening an education system that guarantees equal opportunities for students regardless of race, religion or background.
Among the key initiatives she highlighted was the decision to guarantee matriculation placements for all Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) candidates who obtained 10As.
"Our decision to ensure all 10A students are admitted into matriculation without exception, regardless of race, religion or background, is an important decision for the country.
"Policies that uplift every segment of society without sidelining anyone must be supported and understood in the context of strengthening national education," she said.
Fadhlina said the government's commitment to education extended to all schools, including Chinese vernacular schools, through infrastructure development and continued support.
"There are clear improvements in support for SJKCs, as well as pathways that have been created for students from various educational streams.
"This reflects our commitment to ensuring education is viewed inclusively rather than in silos," she said.
She said the administration would continue to uphold the National Education Philosophy, the Rukun Negara, as well as the Malay language and history as core pillars of the education system.
At the same time, she said the government remained committed to ensuring equitable access to education for all students.
Fadhlina also touched on preparations for the implementation of the 2027 curriculum, which will be fully rolled out for Year One and Form One students next year following Cabinet approval.
She said the curriculum would place greater emphasis on character development alongside academic achievement.
"We want our children to become a generation that is not only academically outstanding but also possesses strong character, empathy and integrity," she said.
Fadhlina said education infrastructure must continue to keep pace with rising enrolment and evolving learning needs.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Visually impaired fisherman beaten to death with log
Article:
TAWAU: A visually impaired fisherman was beaten to death with a mangrove log during an argument while repairing a house in Felda Umas near here on Thursday morning.
In the 10.30am incident, Mohamad Sukry Mobing, 40, was allegedly assaulted by two foreigners who berated him for being slow with the repair work.
The victim, who suffered severe injuries to his head, mouth, and nose, was found covered in blood at the Felda Umas public jetty before being rushed to the local health clinic.
He was subsequently referred to Tawau Hospital by ambulance but was pronounced dead upon arrival at 1.35pm.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Jasmin Hussin said initial investigations showed that the victim, a registered person with disabilities (PwD), was involved in an argument with the two suspects while working on a resident's house.
"According to witness statements, the dispute broke out after the suspects rebuked the victim for his slow progress, leading them to strike him with a mangrove log," he said when contacted today.
Jasmin added that a police team from the Tawau district Criminal Investigation Department tracked down and arrested two foreign men, aged 23 and 52, on Bingkulan Island in Kalabakan to assist in the investigation.
The post-mortem is underway at the Tawau Hospital forensics department to determine the exact cause of death.
"The case is being investigated under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: No reason to bring down Aminuddin-led Negri govt
Article:
SEREMBAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has questioned the motives behind attempts to topple the Negri Sembilan government led by Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
The Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman stressed that Aminuddin faces no allegations of abuse of power or integrity issues, meaning there is no justification to trigger a political crisis and force a state election.
"Which land did Tok Min (Aminuddin) steal? Which logging project did he take? If there are issues of people being neglected, corruption, or abuse of power, we can discuss it. But what is the reason to bring down this government?" he asked when launching the Negri Sembilan PH election machinery here yesterday.
Anwar, who is also the prime minister, said he has repeatedly reminded all quarters to maintain political stability and allow the government to fulfil its duties to the people.
He said the government's current priorities are addressing the cost of living, channelling aid, and strengthening the economy.
"I ask for patience. We have a responsibility to look after the people's welfare, prices of goods, and distribute Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah, Sara and other aid. This term has not even reached three years, yet some are impatient and want to grab power," he said.
The prime minister also questioned why certain quarters are trying to destabilise Negri Sembilan and Johor, despite the federal government continuously channelling massive development allocations to both states.
"If the federal government ignored Negri Sembilan or Johor, perhaps there would be a reason to be angry. But I have never heard such complaints. So, what exactly are they fighting for?" he asked.
Anwar said the government has approved various strategic projects, including a new port development, the expansion of the Tanjung Pelepas Port (PTP), the Johor Baru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link, the Maharani Energy Gateway, and the redevelopment of Forest City.
"These initiatives involve investments and allocations worth tens of billions of ringgit for economic growth and the people's benefit, he added.
"That is why I am baffled. We work day and night to bring in investments, develop states, and look after the people's welfare, yet there are those who are more interested in finding ways to seize power," he said.
Anwar added that he accepted the trust to govern the country, not to accumulate wealth or chase positions, but to ensure the people benefit from economic development.
"When given a trust, execute it to the best of your ability for the people," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Onn Hafiz's rejection of DAP is not about race, says Khaled
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi's rejection of DAP has nothing to do with race, says Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin.
Khaled, the Umno vice-president, said Barisan Nasional has always positioned itself as a multiracial coalition that includes parties representing the Chinese community.
"He did not say that DAP is Chinese and Chinese is DAP. He was speaking about DAP as a political party," Khaled said after attending the Jiwa Murni Madani programme at SK Johor Kampong.
"From the beginning, BN has had Chinese partners. So, the issue of Chinese support in the state election being affected does not arise," he said.
The Kota Tinggi member of parliament was asked if the Johor BN chairman's stance would affect the coalition's prospects in securing Chinese votes.
In his speech at the launch of the
Johor BN election machinery,
Onn Hafiz said he would not sit at the same table with DAP if BN forms the state government after the upcoming polls.
Separately, Khaled welcomed Pas' decision to work with smaller parties in the Johor polls, describing it as part and parcel of democracy.
"I believe all parties, whether new or old, will take the opportunity to contest whenever there is an election, be it a by-election or a state election.
"As a democratic nation, we welcome this. What is important is for the people to be smart, rational, and choose a party that can truly represent them," he said.
Asked if this would pose a challenge to BN, Khaled said the coalition has navigated numerous elections and remains prepared for any outcome.
"I think we have gone through elections many times, and we are always prepared. We also accept the reality that sometimes we win, and sometimes we lose," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fadhlina coy on extra seats for women in Johor, Negri polls
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: PKR Wanita chief Fadhlina Sidek remained tight-lipped on whether the party would allocate additional seats for women candidates in the upcoming Johor and Negri Sembilan elections.
The Election Commission (EC) has set July 11 as
polling day for the Johor election
, with nomination and early voting fixed for June 27 and July 7 respectively.
For the Negri Sembilan election
, polling will be held on Aug 1, while nomination and early voting are scheduled for July 18 and July 28 respectively.
Approached by reporters after an education-related event here today, Fadhlina said discussions on the matter were still ongoing.
"We are finalising it (the candidate list).
"But what is important is 'lawan tetap lawan'," she said briefly.
PKR has yet to officially announce its candidate list for the two state elections, including the number of seats to be contested by women candidates.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Khaled: BN readiness more important than timing of Johor, Negri polls
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Barisan Nasional's election preparedness will determine its victory in
Johor
and
Negri Sembilan
rather than the timing of the two polls, says Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin.
The Umno vice-president said when the Johor assembly was dissolved on June 1, the matter of concurrent elections did not arise as the Negri Sembilan assembly was still active.
"Whether the polls are held together or not, entering an election for BN is about our capabilities and preparations," he said.
Khaled, who is also defence minister, was speaking after launching the Jiwa Murni Madani programme at SK Johor Kampong.
The Kota Tinggi member of parliament was asked if holding the Johor election separately from Negri Sembilan would give BN an advantage.
The Election Commission on Friday announced the key dates for both state elections.
Johor will go to the polls on July 11, while Negri Sembilan will vote on Aug 1.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Factory worker killed in three-vehicle crash after motorcycle skids into oncoming traffic
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: A factory worker was killed after being struck by two cars in a three-vehicle collision along Jalan Pintasan Semeling here last night.
The victim in the 9pm crash was identified as motorcyclist Muhammad Hafizi Muhammad Rizal, 22, from Penaga, Seberang Perai Utara, Penang.
He died at the scene from severe head injuries.
Kuala Muda district police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said initial investigations revealed that two cars, a Perodua Axia and a Perodua Bezza, driven by two women, were travelling from Lembah Bujang towards Semeling.
"While both cars were moving along the route, a Yamaha 135 LC motorcycle ridden by the victim from the opposite direction suddenly skidded into their lane, causing the rider to fall onto the road.
"Due to the close distance, the drivers of the two cars could not avoid hitting the motorcyclist. The victim suffered severe head injuries and was pronounced dead at the scene," he said today.
Hanyan said the body was sent to the Sultan Abdul Halim Hospital forensic unit here for a post-mortem.
The Bezza driver, a 28-year-old salesperson, and the Axia driver, a 21-year-old student, escaped unhurt.
The case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987, he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 18 Bersatu MPs subjected to disciplinary action rally behind Hamzah at convention
Article:
TANAH MERAH: Eighteen Bersatu MPs who were expelled, suspended or removed from the party will gather in solidarity with Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin at the Reset Convention here this afternoon.
Among those expected are Machang MP Wan Ahmad Fayhsal Wan Ahmad Kamal, Ketereh MP Datuk Khlir Mohd Nor, Padang Rengas MP Capt (Rtd) Datuk Azahari Hasan, Gerik MP Fathul Huzir Ayob and former Tasek Gelugor MP Datuk Wan Saiful Wan Jan, all of whom had faced disciplinary action by Bersatu.
Sungai Besar MP Datuk Muslimin Yahaya, who was not among those expelled or suspended, is also expected to attend.
Khlir said their presence was to show support for Hamzah and the political direction outlined under the Reset initiative.
"We came to show our support for Datuk Seri Hamzah. Many of us have gone through difficult circumstances, but we remain committed to the struggle and principles we believe in," he said.
The gathering is drawing attention as one of the largest public appearances involving leaders and members previously subjected to disciplinary action by Bersatu following internal disputes.
The convention, which will be attended by political leaders, professionals and supporters nationwide, will focus on governance reforms, economic resilience and Malaysia's political future.
Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang is expected to officiate the event.
An estimated 10,000 people are expected to attend at the Kota Harmoni Expo Site later this afternoon.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mindanao earthquake: 5.2-magnitude quake hits Philippines days after deadly disaster
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A moderate earthquake measuring 5.2 on the Richter scale struck Mindanao in the Philippines at 10.05am today.
The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said the quake occurred at a depth of 10km, about 101km south of Koronadal City.
"Initial assessments found no tsunami threat to Malaysia," MetMalaysia said in a statement.
The latest tremor comes days after a magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck southern Philippines, affecting hundreds of thousands of people in Mindanao, with tremors felt in parts of Tawau and Semporna in Sabah.
As of Thursday, the Philippines' National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC) reported 47 deaths, 31 people missing and more than 346,000 people affected by the disaster.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Foreigner with 34 outstanding summonses arrested after fleeing RTD operation
Article:
PENDANG: A foreigner with 34 outstanding traffic summonses was arrested after fleeing a Road Transport Department (RTD) enforcement operation in Guar Kepayang here yesterday afternoon.
In the 6.30pm incident, the Rohingya abandoned his motorcycle and fled through a padi field upon spotting the enforcement officers.
However, the team managed to detain him.
Checks revealed that he had 34 active, unsettled summonses, including for driving without a license.
Kedah RTD director Stien Van Lutam said the motorcycle was seized.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Malaysia’s strong ties and political stability helping secure energy supplies
Article:
SIPITANG, June 13 — Malaysia’s strong international relations and political stability have enabled the country to secure continued access to oil and gas supplies despite growing uncertainty arising from the global energy crisis, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
He said Malaysia’s prudent diplomatic approach had allowed countries such as Russia and Turkmenistan to regard Malaysia as a close and trusted partner.
Recalling his recent official visit to Turkmenistan, Anwar said the country’s leadership had expressed its willingness to collaborate with Malaysia through Petronas in operating one of the world’s largest gas fields.
“Our country maintains friendly relations with all nations, including the United States and China. At the same time, our domestic political situation remains stable, without upheaval or racial discord,” he said when officiating the groundbreaking ceremony of the Sabah Southern Madani Link (SSML) project here today.
Also present were Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali, and Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud.
Anwar said political stability was key to enabling the government to focus on its development agenda while continuing to attract foreign investment.
“That is why I have continued to support Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor. We have had enough disputes over many issues, but the country’s politics must remain stable because without stability, investors will not come,” he said.
Anwar also said that the world was facing growing uncertainty due to energy supply disruptions arising from conflicts in the Middle East, which had also affected shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and driven up global oil prices.
He said the situation had forced several countries, including some Asean members, to take drastic measures, including closing about 400 petrol stations due to supply shortages.
“We are grateful that despite these circumstances, fuel prices remain low... in Malaysia, RON95 remains at RM1.99 per litre.
“We adjusted diesel prices slightly, but not for sectors such as agriculture, buses and public transportation because we must ensure that, whatever the challenge, the burden does not fall on the people,” he said. — Bernama

Title: New projects should include long-term maintenance plans, says Hannah Yeoh
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — Federal Territories Minister Hannah Yeoh said governments needed to devote more attention and resources to maintaining existing public facilities, adding that the long-term costs of upkeep should be factored into decisions on new developments.
Speaking at the launch of Kuala Lumpur Sustainability Week (KLSW) today, she said the challenge was not merely constructing new amenities, but ensuring they could continue serving the public years after completion.
According to
The Star
, Yeoh said proposals for new developments should be accompanied by clear plans for their long-term maintenance.
“When people bring proposals to me and talk about creating something new, I am always more concerned about the budget required to maintain it,” she said.
Yeoh said Malaysia’s weather conditions could accelerate the deterioration of buildings, parks and other public infrastructure if maintenance was neglected.
“If there is no plan to sustain and maintain them, it will all go to waste. That is why it is important for the government to look into our spending priorities,” she said.
She said Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) and the Federal Territories Department were focusing on maintaining green spaces and strengthening flood mitigation measures as part of efforts to improve sustainability in the capital.
Yeoh added that investments in infrastructure and heritage conservation would be undermined without adequate flood prevention measures.
Referring to the ongoing conservation of the Sultan Abdul Samad Building, she said preservation efforts needed to be supported by flood mitigation planning.
“However, if we do not have a flood mitigation plan for that area, it will mean nothing and we will end up spending all over again if it gets flooded,” she said.

Title: Low UNHCR resettlement rate contributing to prolonged Rohingya presence in Malaysia, says Saifuddin Nasution
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, June 13 — The low rate of third-country resettlement by the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) is among the factors contributing to the prolonged presence of refugees, including the Rohingya, within local communities.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said that while UNHCR has two core responsibilities - registering refugees and securing third-country resettlement — statistics show that the number of refugee cards issued by the agency far exceeds the number of individuals successfully resettled abroad.
He said the imbalance has resulted in Malaysia having to deal with the issue, including the presence of Rohingya refugees, who continue to live alongside local communities while awaiting uncertain resettlement opportunities.
“UNHCR’s core responsibility is to register refugees and then find a third country for resettlement. However, the number of successful third-country placements is far lower than the number of UNHCR cards issued by the agency itself. As a result, while waiting to be resettled, these refugees remain within local communities,” he said.
He said this in response to the issue of the ‘Kempen Tangkap Rohingya’ (Catch Rohingya Campaign) circulating on social media, after attending the Drug-Free Aspirations Tour (JABD) 2026 programme here.
Touching on the growing momentum of the campaign and calls by netizens on social media for firm action against the group, he said the public has a right to voice their views as the issue has a direct impact on the country’s economic, security and social landscape.
However, he said, refugees have a different status from undocumented migrants, who can be dealt with directly under the Immigration Act, as refugees are driven from their home countries by conflict and other push factors.
To address the security implications arising from the situation, he said the government is currently undertaking its own data collection exercise and no longer relies entirely on UNHCR data.
“Once we have our own data, we can take enforcement action should any of them violate the country’s laws. They remain subject to domestic laws, and firm action will be taken against those who breach the country’s regulations,” he said.
He said the issue is a longstanding one and rejected attempts by certain parties to place the blame for the large refugee population entirely on the current government.
According to him, the government remains committed to managing the issue based on security considerations and humanitarian principles, while welcoming reasonable and constructive proposals from academics and social activists to help resolve the matter. — Bernama

Title: Anwar orders swift rollout of RM250,000 aid for Sipitang schools, rejects project delays
Article:
SIPITANG, June 13 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today approved an immediate special allocation of RM250,000 for development purposes, including repair works on basic facilities at secondary schools in the Sipitang district.
He said details on the implementation of the assistance and the disbursement of the funds would be coordinated immediately by the Implementation Coordination Unit of the Prime Minister’s Department.
“For school facilities, I am approving RM250,000 immediately. I do not want a project to be approved in June 2026 and only begin in August 2027. That is an outdated attitude,” he said when officiating at the groundbreaking ceremony of the Sabah Southern MADANI Link project here today.
Also present were Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali, and Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud.
In this regard, the Prime Minister wants representatives from the relevant agencies to visit the affected schools within two weeks to assess their needs and process the assistance for the comfort of educators and students.
Anwar also called on all Sabahans to cooperate in ensuring that the close relationship and cooperation between the federal and state governments remain strong for the continuity of development.
He explained that political stability at both the state and federal levels is the most important pillar in enabling the government to focus fully on its agenda of improving the welfare of the people.
Anwar stressed that the time had come to end prolonged political disputes as they would only harm the state and deter foreign investment.
“That is why I think enough is enough with all these disputes over various issues. The country’s politics must be stable. If there is no stability, people will not invest and we cannot focus on development,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Zahid defends Umno’s solo bid in Negeri Sembilan, rejects betrayal claims
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — Umno has rejected allegations of betrayal levelled by Negeri Sembilan caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, with party president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi saying the party’s support for Aminuddin’s administration was only intended to last until the dissolution of the state assembly.
According to
Utusan Malaysia
, Zahid said there had been no act of betrayal on the part of Negeri Sembilan Umno, as the party’s backing for the menteri besar had been given only until the end of the state’s term.
He said that after Aminuddin announced the dissolution of the Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly on June 5, Umno decided to contest the upcoming state election independently.
“We work as a coalition of parties that naturally has its own direction, which must be translated into action.
“I do not think there has been any betrayal when the term of a state administration or the federal government has come to an end,” he told reporters after officiating the 4th Southeast Asian Scholars Conference 2026 here today.
Zahid said Umno’s leadership was focused on finding the best way to strengthen the party’s position, whether in the state assembly or in Parliament.
“Once the state assembly has been dissolved, we seek the best approach to reinforce our position in the state assembly or in Parliament,” he said.
His remarks came a day after Aminuddin accused Umno of betraying its allies and urged voters to punish the party in the Negeri Sembilan state election on August 1.
Speaking at the launch of Pakatan Harapan’s Negeri Sembilan election machinery, Aminuddin called on supporters to turn out in large numbers on polling day, saying voter turnout should reach 80 per cent.
“Show our dissatisfaction towards those who betrayed us. If possible, we do not want just around 70 per cent voter turnout on August 1. We want 80 per cent of voters to cast their ballots.
“This is our opportunity. This is our moment. Show them. Teach them a lesson. Make sure that the people today are able to mete out punishment to them. Those who can deliver that punishment are the voters,” he said.
Election Commission chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun announced yesterday that polling for the Negeri Sembilan election will be held on August 1, with nominations set for July 18 and early voting on July 28.

Title: Malaysian Judiciary warns of fake debt notice threatening bank account freeze
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — The Malaysian Judiciary has warned the public against a fake document circulating online that falsely claims to have been issued by the Office of the Chief Registrar of the Federal Court of Malaysia.
In a statement posted on its official Facebook page today, the judiciary said it had received complaints regarding a forged notice bearing the title “Final Warning for the Freezing of All Savings and Current Accounts, and Restrictions on EPF Withdrawals under the Debt Claims Act 1957”.
The notice purportedly warned recipients that their bank accounts would be frozen, company accounts registered with the Companies Commission of Malaysia (SSM) restricted, and Employees Provident Fund (EPF) withdrawals blocked over alleged outstanding debts.
The judiciary stressed that the document was fraudulent and had not been issued by the Office of the Chief Registrar.
“The Office of the Chief Registrar of the Federal Court of Malaysia confirms that the document is fake and was not issued by this office,” it said.
The forged letter, which carried the letterhead of the Office of the Chief Registrar and cited the Debt Claims Act 1957, threatened legal action unless payment amounting to RM77,952.42 was made within 24 hours.
The judiciary urged members of the public to exercise caution if they receive similar documents.
“Members of the public are advised to be careful if they receive documents of this nature,” it said.
Those seeking clarification or wishing to verify the authenticity of court-related communications may contact the judiciary at komunikasikorporat@kehakiman.gov.my.

Title: Zahid calls on Muslim scholars to turn ideas into policies and solutions for the ummah
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has called on Muslim scholars to spearhead an ‘Intellectual Diplomacy’ agenda that actively translates consensus into concrete policy and real-world solutions for the ummah.
He said such diplomacy must bridge the gap between academic theory and practical governance amid a global landscape marked by economic uncertainty, geopolitical tensions and a profound crisis of values, where wisdom is increasingly being sidelined by emotion.
“If this Intellectual Diplomacy agenda can be realised, I believe Southeast Asia can offer a model of Islam that is knowledgeable, civilised and relevant to the challenges of our time.
“A model that is not extreme. A model that is not detached from reality. A model that embraces progress while preserving human values,” he said at the opening of the 4th Southeast Asian Scholars Conference 2026, co-organised by the Muslim World League and the Department of Islamic Development Malaysia (Jakim) here today.
Ahmad Zahid, who is also the Minister of Rural and Regional Development, said Southeast Asia, with its moderate Islamic tradition and diverse societies, is uniquely positioned to offer an Ethical Technology Model that embraces progress without eroding identity.
Pointing to Malaysia’s success in producing professional huffaz who excel in modern fields such as medicine, engineering and finance, Ahmad Zahid said national development must prioritise human capital over purely physical growth.
He said the Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) agenda would continue to be strengthened as a strategic pathway to provide young Muslims and huffaz with opportunities in high-demand economic sectors.
On regional cooperation, Ahmad Zahid called on Southeast Asian scholars to build a united front against cross-border threats such as digital extremism and misinformation, while continuing to advocate humanitarian causes, including the plight of the Palestinian people.
He also highlighted the rapid advancement of artificial intelligence (AI), describing it as a double-edged sword that requires strong ethical guidance.
“My greatest concern is that technology without values can become a tool of manipulation. Data without ethics can violate human dignity. Algorithms without wisdom can silently shape the minds of society,” he said.
Therefore, he said Maqasid Syariah should serve as an ethical compass for evaluating innovations based on their ability to protect religion, life, intellect, lineage and property. — Bernama

Title: Tiong: Expand community engagement programmes to strengthen public understanding of military’s role
Article:
SIBU,  June 13 — Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing has called on the Malaysian Army to expand community engagement programmes to strengthen public understanding of the military’s role and foster patriotism.
He said initiatives such as the Jelajah Wira Madani programme should be expanded as they help the public better appreciate the sacrifices and contributions of military personnel in safeguarding the nation’s sovereignty.
“The peace and stability we enjoy today are the result of the sacrifices and unwavering commitment of our security personnel, who are always prepared to defend the nation.
“I hope more programmes of this nature will be organised in the future, including those involving male and female military veterans,” he said at the Gawai Day celebration held in conjunction with the Jelajah Wira Madani 2026 programme at Dewan RASCOM, RASCOM Camp 9th  Brigade, here today.
At the event, Tiong also presented an allocation of RM50,000 to the 9th Brigade.
The Jelajah Wira Madani programme is an initiative by the government and the Ministry of Defence to engage with and recognise the contributions of frontline personnel, particularly members of the Malaysian Army.
Tiong, who is also Bintulu MP and assemblyman for Dudong, said Gawai Day holds special significance for the Dayak community in Sarawak as it symbolises gratitude for the harvest and blessings received, as well as unity among the state’s diverse communities.
He said unity remains a valuable asset that underpins stability, economic progress and national development.
“Sarawak’s cultural and ethnic diversity should continue to be a strength in building a better future,” he said.
Tiong also expressed appreciation to members of the 9th Brigade, their families and military veterans for their service and sacrifices in preserving the country’s peace and sovereignty.
Also present was Army Eastern Field Commander Lt Gen Datuk Mohd Sofi Md Lepi. — Bernama

Title: Saifuddin Nasution says Poisons Act updates must keep pace with emerging synthetic drugs
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, June 13 — Authorities and regulators face a growing challenge to fast-track the scheduling of synthetic drug chemicals under the Poisons Act 1952, a move critical to ensuring unimpeded legal action.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said swift action was critical, warning that delays in gazetting the substances would severely hamper police investigations.
“When a police investigation traces a case back to synthetic drugs, the matter is referred to the Chemistry Department. If toxic elements are identified but happen to be missing from the poisons registry, it severely complicates any subsequent legal proceedings,” he said.
He was speaking at a press conference after attending the Aspirasi Bebas Dadah Tour, organised by the National Anti-Drugs Agency (AADK), at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Puteri Wangsa today.
He was responding to comments by Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay, who argued that the emergence of a new synthetic drug known as “Piu Piu,” which was found laced in e-cigarette liquids, justifies a total ban on vapes.
Ayob Khan said this was because the drug contains a mixture of Fentanyl and psychoactive chemicals, which can trigger a severe, zombie-like high in users.
Saifuddin Nasution said the issue of classifying toxic substances has already been brought to the Cabinet for discussion to find a permanent solution.
In response, the Home Ministry is stepping up coordination with the Ministry of Health (MOH) and the Chemistry Department to ensure more effective law enforcement against new synthetic drug syndicates.
He further revealed that national drug data mapping identifies the country’s East Coast as the hardest-hit area for synthetic drug use, with the trend starting in Kelantan and Terengganu before moving into Pahang.
He noted that while the West Coast still records the use of mixed substances such as marijuana and heroin, the East Coast is now dominated by synthetic drugs, which have a far more devastating and alarming impact.
He said the country has recorded alarming statistics in domestic security, with a total of 192,000 individuals confirmed to be involved in drug and substance abuse nationwide, according to an integrated data profile combining records from the MOH, private rehabilitation centres, AADK rehab clinics, and the police narcotics division.
Based on the overall data, the national average now stands at 560 drug abusers per 100,000 residents, with consumption trends heavily dominated by dangerous synthetic drugs.
Commenting on the situation in Johor, he said the state recorded an average of 499 people involved in drug abuse per 100,000 residents.
Although the state average sits below the national benchmark, he said Mersing recorded concerning figures, reaching the highest rate in Johor at 1,200 individuals per 100,000 residents, followed by Pontian, Kota Tinggi, and Segamat.
He said there are currently 30 drug rehabilitation centres nationwide managed by AADK, which currently house 5,000 clients. — Bernama

Title: Johor voters should judge candidates by service and commitment, says Azalina
Article:
KOTA TINGGI, June 13 — Umno information chief Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said has urged voters to choose candidates who are close to the people, capable of providing the best service and who place the interests of their constituencies as a priority in the Johor state election on July 11.
Azalina, who is also Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform), said voters should assess the track record and commitment of candidates before making their choice, as elected representatives who do not focus on their constituencies would make it more difficult to safeguard the welfare of local residents.
